channel.title: Tutor — Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy
channel.description: A weekly 20–30 minute two-host walk through the past week of wrist ARTHROSCOPY literature — arthroscopic techniques for scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate & lunotriquetral ligament injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, and cartilage injuries. Key studies, corresponding authors, and clinically relevant takeaways. Generated automatically from PubMed.
channel.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
channel.itunes:summary: A weekly 20–30 minute two-host walk through the past week of wrist ARTHROSCOPY literature — arthroscopic techniques for scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate & lunotriquetral ligament injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, and cartilage injuries. Key studies, corresponding authors, and clinically relevant takeaways. Generated automatically from PubMed.

item.title: Wrist Arthroscopy — 2026-07-03
item.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
item.description: Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy Literature Digest — 2026-07-03 Pulled from PubMed across the six target journals — JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics. Restricted to articles focused on wrist arthroscopic techniques (arthroscopic treatment of scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate/lunotriquetral injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, cartilage injuries, etc.). Author line = first & senior author; corresponding-author institution listed per article. 18 articles (this first run widened to a ~18-month window for a fuller sample; going forward this runs weekly over the prior 7 days). JBJS Am and Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery had no qualifying arthroscopic-technique wrist articles in this window. --- Hand Clinics (7) A themed issue on open-versus-arthroscopic wrist surgery. Wrist Anatomy: Open and Arthroscopic Surgical Approaches Emanuels AF & Wu KY — Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN, USA A practical review of volar and dorsal carpal anatomy as seen through open and arthroscopic approaches, framing how the choice of approach in the young arthritic wrist is dictated by the pathology and the exposure needed to work safely. PubMed Arthroscopic Treatment of Thumb Carpometacarpal Joint Arthritis Bougioukli S & Chung KC — University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI, USA A review of arthroscopic-assisted management of basilar thumb arthritis, arguing that wrist-arthroscopy techniques address several shortcomings of open trapeziectomy while the optimal procedure remains unsettled. PubMed Total Wrist Arthrodesis: Open Versus Arthroscopic Alsayed A & Awan H — The Ohio State University, Columbus, OH, USA A review of indications and techniques for total wrist fusion in advanced rheumatoid or post-traumatic arthritis (including SLAC/SNAC), comparing open and arthroscopic approaches and stressing careful patient selection given the trade of motion for pain relief. PubMed Proximal Row Carpectomy: Open Versus Arthroscopy Lim RQR & Chim H — Louisiana State University Health Sciences Center, New Orleans, LA, USA A review of arthroscopic proximal row carpectomy as a motion-preserving salvage option, weighing the promise of less soft-tissue injury and faster rehab against a real learning curve and longer operative time. PubMed Atypical Fusions Radioscapholunate/Lunotriquetral: Open vs Arthroscopic Tse S & Bayne CO — University of California, Davis, Sacramento, CA, USA A review of RSL and LT limited fusions for arthritis/instability, noting that distal scaphoid and triquetral excision can improve motion and that arthroscopic approaches improve visualization at the cost of a steep learning curve. PubMed Current Trends in 4-Corner and 3-Corner Fusions: Comparing Open and Arthroscopic Approaches Paul R & Persitz J — Toronto Western Hospital, University Health Network, Toronto, Canada A review of 4- and 3-corner fusions for SLAC/SNAC art
